reported_papers.title: Cognition to Control - Multi-Agent Learning for Human-Humanoid Collaborative Transport
reported_papers.reason: 误命中：认知到控制 C2C 三层层次结构用于人 - 人形机器人协作运输。聚焦 HRC 而非 VLA 核心方法，与 VLA 架构/训练距离较远。

reported_papers.title: MEM: Multi-Scale Embodied Memory for Vision Language Action Models
reported_papers.reason: 提出多尺度具身记忆 MEM，结合长期语义记忆与短期事件记忆。长期记忆捕获抽象语义概念，短期记忆补偿遮挡，解决长视野多阶段真实任务的多粒度记忆表示瓶颈。 [Pass3降级: VLA 记忆模块方向已高度饱和，且与近期 TacMamba 历史压缩主题重复。]

reported_papers.title: LiteVLA-Edge: Quantized On-Device Multimodal Control for Embedded Robotics
reported_papers.reason: 面向部署的 VLA 流水线 LiteVLA-Edge，支持 Jetson Orin 完全设备端推理。结合 FP32 微调与 4-bit GGUF 量化，端到端延迟 150.5ms(6.6Hz)，ROS 2 集成完全离线运行。 [Pass3降级: 与 3 天前推荐的 QuantVLA 主题重复，且主要依赖现有量化工具集成。]

reported_papers.title: RoboCasa365: A Large-Scale Simulation Framework for Training and Benchmarking Generalist Robots
reported_papers.reason: 提出 RoboCasa365 综合仿真基准，365 个日常任务覆盖 2500 个多样化厨房环境。600+ 小时人类演示数据 +1600+ 小时合成数据，支持通用策略系统评估，填补大规模可复现基准空白。

Existing learning-based methods rely heavily on supervised training with high-quality 3D data and manual annotations, limiting scalability and diversity. reported_papers.title: TRACE: Object Motion Editing in Videos with First-Frame Trajectory Guidance
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: We study object motion path editing in videos, where the goal is to alter a target object's trajectory while preserving the original scene content. Unlike prior video editing methods that primarily manipulate appearance or rely on point-track-based trajectory control, which is often challenging for users to provide during inference, especially in videos with camera motion, we offer a practical, easy-to-use approach to controllable object-centric motion editing. reported_papers.title: Out of Sight but Not Out of Mind: Hybrid Memory for Dynamic Video World Models
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: Video world models have shown immense potential in simulating the physical world, yet existing memory mechanisms primarily treat environments as static canvases. When dynamic subjects hide out of sight and later re-emerge, current methods often struggle, leading to frozen, distorted, or vanishing subjects. reported_papers.title: How Far Are Vision-Language Models from Constructing the Real World? A Benchmark for Physical Generative Reasoning
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: The physical world is not merely visual; it is governed by rigorous structural and procedural constraints. Yet, the evaluation of vision-language models (VLMs) remains heavily skewed toward perceptual realism, prioritizing the generation of visually plausible 3D layouts, shapes, and appearances. reported_papers.title: Evaluating Language Models for Harmful Manipulation
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: Interest in the concept of AI-driven harmful manipulation is growing, yet current approaches to evaluating it are limited. This paper introduces a framework for evaluating harmful AI manipulation via context-specific human-AI interaction studies. We illustrate the utility of this framework by assessing an AI model with 10,101 participants spanning interactions in three AI use domains (public policy, finance, and health) and three locales (US, UK, and India). reported_papers.title: Modernising Reinforcement Learning-Based Navigation for Embodied Semantic Scene Graph Generation
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: Semantic world models enable embodied agents to reason about objects, relations, and spatial context beyond purely geometric representations. In Organic Computing, such models are a key enabler for objective-driven self-adaptation under uncertainty and resource constraints. reported_papers.title: R-C2: Cycle-Consistent Reinforcement Learning Improves Multimodal Reasoning
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: Robust perception and reasoning require consistency across sensory modalities. Yet current multimodal models often violate this principle, yielding contradictory predictions for visual and textual representations of the same concept. reported_papers.title: Learning to Staff: Offline Reinforcement Learning and Fine-Tuned LLMs for Warehouse Staffing Optimization
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: We investigate machine learning approaches for optimizing real-time staffing decisions in semi-automated warehouse sortation systems. reported_papers.title: Train at Moving Edge: Online-Verified Prompt Selection for Efficient RL Training of Large Reasoning Model
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become essential for post-training large language models (LLMs) in reasoning tasks. While scaling rollouts can stabilize training and enhance performance, the computational overhead is a critical issue. reported_papers.title: Offline Decision Transformers for Neural Combinatorial Optimization: Surpassing Heuristics on the Traveling Salesman Problem
reported_papers.abstract_snippet: Combinatorial optimization problems like the Traveling Salesman Problem are critical in industry yet NP-hard.
